name: LibreComputer Alta (AML-A311D-CC)
description: Amlogic A311D Cottonwood-series SBC with 5 TOPS NPU and Pi-compatible 40-pin header

release-date.comment: Pre-orders began September/October 2023; first shipments November 24, 2023.

status.comment: Still actively sold by Libre Computer and resellers (LoveRPi, Amazon) and listed with current Armbian images as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Per Libre Computer Project policy, schematics and 2D silkscreen are released, but full PCB CAD files are not. Per the SBC RESEARCH.md guideline, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW.

country-of-origin.comment: Designed by Shenzhen Libre Technology Co., Ltd., Shenzhen, China.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Cortex-A73 cluster up to 2.2 GHz; A53 cluster up to 1.8 GHz.

process-node.comment: Vendor (Amlogic) does not publish the node directly; widely reported as 12 nm in third-party SoC databases.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G52 hardware advertises OpenGL ES 3.2; the open-source Panfrost driver in mainline Mesa is conformant up to OpenGL ES 3.1 on G52.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G52 hardware supports Vulkan 1.1, but the open-source PanVk driver is conformant only on Mali-G610; on G52 it is non-conformant/work-in-progress, and Amlogic does not ship a vendor Vulkan driver for this board. Practical Vulkan support is therefore not available as of 2026-04.

hw-video-decode.comment: Amlogic A311D AVE-10 video engine officially supports H.264, H.265 (10-bit), and VP9 (Profile-2 4K@60). No AV1 decode.

hw-video-encode.comment: Amlogic datasheet specifies H.264 and H.265 encode up to 1080p@60. No VP9/AV1 encode.

emmc.comment: Onboard eMMC 5.x slim connector for a removable eMMC module (modules sold separately).

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; Libre Computer's stated design choice — users are expected to attach USB Wi-Fi or M.2-equivalent dongles.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth (paired with the lack of Wi-Fi).

usb-c-ports-data.comment: Single USB-C port: power + USB 2.0 OTG data with USB-PD.

poe-support.comment: PoE pin header on board accepts an external PoE module/HAT; PoE is not available without an add-on, so per the RESEARCH.md guideline this maps to `via-hat`.

hdmi-outputs.comment: Single HDMI 2.0 (some marketing materials list "HDMI 2.1" but the SoC's HDMI block is 2.0-class).

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.0 output supports 4K@60; MIPI-DSI is limited to 1080p.

displayport.comment: USB-C is USB 2.0 OTG only — no DisplayPort Alt-Mode.

mipi-dsi.comment: 4-lane MIPI-DSI on a 22-pin connector, up to 1080p.

mipi-csi.comment: 4-lane MIPI-CSI on a 22-pin connector (supports up to two 8 MP cameras with split lanes).

audio-jack.comment: 3.5 mm combo jack carrying analog stereo audio plus CVBS composite video.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin GPIO header pin-compatible with the Raspberry Pi 3B+; Libre Computer markets it as a drop-in upgrade path.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Same dimensions as a Raspberry Pi 3B+ with matching mounting holes and header position; physical HAT compatibility is good. EEPROM-based HAT auto-config is not advertised, so this is mechanical HAT compatibility (the common bar for the SBC market).

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: The A311D's PCIe lane is consumed internally by the onboard USB 3.0 hub/Ethernet chain; no PCIe is broken out to the user.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, UART, and SPDIF are all available via the 40-pin header.

power-input.value: USB-C 5V (USB-PD), PoE module, or 5V GPIO header
power-input.comment: Three input options. USB-PD supported on the Type-C port; vendor recommends a supply with 5.0–5.5 V regulation.

idle-power-w.comment: Measured at idle in bret.dk review (2024). Vendor's "1 W idle" marketing number could not be reproduced under typical Linux idle conditions.

load-power-w.comment: Peak measured at full load with active fan cooling. With heatsink only, the SoC throttles and load draw drops to ~4.8 W.

cooling-required.comment: Ships with a pre-applied integrated heatsink. Sustained full load throttles after ~2 minutes without active airflow, so an active fan is preferable for sustained max-load workloads, but the vendor's stock configuration is heatsink-only.

rtc-battery-header.comment: No dedicated RTC battery header is documented on the board.

length-mm.comment: Bret.dk hands-on measured 85.6 × 56.5 mm — slightly larger than the 85 × 56 mm Pi-standard reference but within Pi-standard tolerance.

form-factor-class.comment: 85.6 × 56.5 mm with Pi-style mounting holes; within ±5 mm of Pi-standard 85 × 56 mm threshold from RESEARCH.md.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Pi 3B+-style 4-corner mounting hole pattern (Libre Computer markets the board as a Pi 3B+ replacement).

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight is not published by the vendor. Amazon lists 68 g shipping weight, but that includes packaging — not used here.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: A311D has long-standing mainline support; the Alta board boots stock upstream kernels with Ethernet, USB, HDMI, GPU (Panfrost OpenGL ES), and GPIO functional. NPU device-tree support is landing in mainline kernel 6.17+, so NPU acceleration via mainline is `partial` even though core peripherals are `full`.

official-os-image.comment: Libre Computer publishes maintained Ubuntu, Debian, and Raspberry-Pi-OS-based images for the board.

distros-with-images.comment: Armbian lists the Alta with Standard support (Ubuntu Noble, Debian Trixie, etc.). Libre Computer's own images are Ubuntu/Debian/RPi-OS-based. Fedora and openSUSE are advertised as compatible via SystemReady-IR but do not publish board-specific images, so they are excluded per RESEARCH.md guidance.

bootloader.comment: Stock firmware is mainline U-Boot, packaged so that the OS sees it as Arm SystemReady-IR / UEFI. Both labels are accurate.

long-term-support.comment: Vendor does not publish a formal support-end date, but Libre Computer's track record on prior Cottonwood boards (Le Potato, Renegade) is multi-year mainline tracking. Estimated 3-5y based on the company's pattern; downgrade if the company publishes a tighter window.

android-support.comment: Libre Computer has not published an official Android image for the Alta as of 2026-04. Android sources for A311D exist via Khadas VIM3 / Amlogic SDK, but no maintained image targets this board specifically.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Launch MSRP November 2023 was $60. Resellers (Amazon, LoveRPi) typically list at $65-90 depending on bundle. Price checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Single SKU — only one RAM/storage configuration is sold, so base and top MSRP are identical.

available-regions.comment: Sold via Amazon US, Amazon UK, LoveRPi (US), AliExpress, and direct Libre Computer / Shenzhen channels. Availability snapshot 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at most resellers as of 2026-04-26 (LoveRPi was sold out at one snapshot but Amazon listings remained live).

documentation-quality.comment: Vendor hub aggregates schematics, GPIO pinout, and OS guides; SoC datasheet is publicly available. Falls short of polished, single-page docs (à la Raspberry Pi); information is spread across forum posts.

community-size.comment: Libre Computer has an active but smaller user base than the major SBC vendors; per RESEARCH.md thresholds this maps to `small`.

active-forum-discord.comment: hub.libre.computer Discourse forum has regular activity from both users and Libre Computer staff.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Mostly leans on generic Pi 3B+-compatible cases and HATs (header is mechanically Pi-compatible). Few first-party accessories beyond an eMMC module and a PoE/fan HAT; no official display, camera, or branded ecosystem of the size Pi or Khadas/Hardkernel maintain.
